ranked_movies.title: Dancer in the Dark
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as the ultimate bleak and impactful film that leaves viewers emotionally devastated, described as watching 'a kind, innocent person get absolutely curb stomped by life'.

ranked_movies.title: Requiem for a Dream
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently mentioned across multiple threads as a profoundly disturbing and soul-crushing film that leaves a lasting impact.

ranked_movies.title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
ranked_movies.reason: Highly praised for its timeless dialogue, perfect character writing, and powerful performances, considered a top-tier classic that holds up perfectly today.

ranked_movies.title: The Godfather Part II
ranked_movies.reason: Celebrated as a masterclass in 'show, don't tell' filmmaking with Al Pacino's subtle, eye-driven performance being particularly highlighted.

ranked_movies.title: Manchester by the Sea
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a movie that will 'destroy' the viewer with its profound and heartbreaking story.

ranked_movies.title: Beau Is Afraid
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned multiple times for its ability to make the audience feel the main character's anxiety and discomfort, creating an intensely unsettling experience.

ranked_movies.title: Aftersun
ranked_movies.reason: Deeply analyzed for its exploration of memory, parental relationships, and depression, praised for its subtlety and emotional power.

ranked_movies.title: Nightcrawler
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for making viewers uncomfortable through its brilliantly written and acted despicable main character.

ranked_movies.title: Threads
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a movie that will completely shatter the viewer's beliefs and require significant processing time afterwards.

ranked_movies.title: Arrival
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a prime example of a thoughtful alien movie that focuses more on thriller and abduction elements than sci-fi/horror.

ranked_movies.title: Wallace and Gromit: The Wrong Trousers
ranked_movies.reason: Hailed as one of the greatest clay animation films ever made, with specific praise for its iconic train chase scene.

ranked_movies.title: There Will Be Blood
ranked_movies.reason: Referenced in discussion about films that grow on viewers after multiple viewings, considered a top-tier masterpiece.

ranked_movies.title: Tampopo
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a smart, funny, profound, and joyful film that celebrates food and life while saying much about the human condition.

ranked_movies.title: The Shining
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as a prime example of a film where the main character slowly unravels, showcasing psychological deterioration.

ranked_movies.title: Singin' in the Rain
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a beautiful, joyful film that captivates viewers while being important cinema history.

ranked_movies.title: The Girl Next Door
ranked_movies.reason: Praised as a fun, great little movie with surprising heart and moral fiber, despite its silly premise.

ranked_movies.title: Under the Skin
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a unique alien movie that creates unsettling thrills rather than typical sci-fi/horror.

ranked_movies.title: Falling Down
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned multiple times as a film showcasing a main character's gradual unraveling and descent.

ranked_movies.title: The Straight Story
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as David Lynch's understated, sincere, and uplifting film about family love and redemption.

ranked_movies.title: The Secret of NIMH
ranked_movies.reason: Recalled as a childhood-terrifying film that still holds up as timeless and quality animation.
